string.text: Just you keep walkingģ YouÆll remember this moment, thatÆs for sureģ
Hey Beard, check out what I found in that old tub. Will this do?
Wowģ thatÆs something else. IÆve never seen one of these before! Here, take this.
Thank you. If you have any more work, you know where to find me.
Oh, itÆs you. Well, I need cash as much as you do.
Stop right there!!! Holster your weapon and donÆt you move!!!
Stay still and donÆt even think about moving, asshole! One wrong move and youÆre toast!
Good, now turn around, slowly, and piss off back to where you came from!
Are you dumb or something?! Get outta here!!!
Hey douche bag, is anyone even there? Scram or IÆll drop you right frigginÆ now!!!
IÆm going to bleed you dry, asshole!
Ice, get to the roof. The rest of you follow me.
Ready to attack on your order.
TheyÆll spot you like that, Major.
LetÆs wait until they get to the center of the yardģ
We must act before weÆre spotted. Open fire!
LetÆs check them out. Ice, cover us.

We thank you, O Monolith, for revealing the cunning plans of Your enemies to us. May Your Light shine down on the souls of the brave soldiers who gave their lives in service to Your Will! Onward, warriors of the Monolith! Avenge your fallen brothers, blessed as they are in their eternal union with the Monolith. Bring death to those whoÆve spurned the holy power of the Monolith!
Strange. Is someone leaking our plans to them? We should take this to Kovalsky.
What do I do? If I go, IÆll lose moneyģ if I donÆt go, IÆll end up deadģ
Would you look at that! The man himself!
DonÆt let the sentries see you.
What have you got? Show me.
Look, weÆve got these here artifacts and we can get more of them. In exchange weÆre looking for weapons and some good equipment.
You know that IÆve got whatever DutyÆs got, so weapons and equipment are not a problem. All my stuff is top quality, no doubt about it.
Why donÆt we have a little chat, you and I?
I think I found their lair. Get over here, ASAP.
I saw a bloodsucker enter the building. IÆm guessing thatÆs their lair, but letÆs put that theory to the test.
Watch the shadows, they could appear from anywhere.
Did you hear that? Never mind, I must be hearing things.
Come look at this ¢ itÆs probably the only way of getting in the basement.
Well, jumping down is the only way! Follow me!
Your footsies still intact? LetÆs check out this basement then.
This is what I do. IÆve been picking locks all my life.
Please, hold your applause. Now let's take a look inside.
Shhh! ItÆs full of sleeping bloodsuckers! LetÆs try to get by them without making any noise.
PhewģWe made it and didnÆt even disturb their nap time!
This looks like an exit! Jump!
We need to tell Beard about thisģ LetÆs hope heÆll know how to deal with these bastards.

We didnÆt find the hunter, but I have an idea of where he might be. Let me check it out and IÆll contact you. While I do that, let the stalkers know about these bloodsucking assholes ¢ something needs to be done about them.
Holster that shooter, stalker, or IÆm putting you right in the ground!
What did you do that for? He didnÆt touch you, did he? Damn!
Get up here! Things are about to kick off!
Come on, what are you waiting for?! YouÆll miss the best part!
What are you doing? Hurry up, the fun stuff is about to start!
LetÆs see if I was right.
Looks like I was right after allģ Now the zombies are gonna cover us. Shoot those bastards down!
Looks like I was right after allģ Shoot those bastards down!
So thatÆs thatģ I can see you're bursting with questions and I can understand that. LetÆs have a little talk, shall we?
So, did you find anything out about Magpie?
That son of a bitch is going to regret running away.
Hang in there, buddy. The doc said youÆll be up on your feet in a couple of days. You might as well use this time to rest up and chillģ

Listen up. The chimera hunts at night and returns to its lair in the morning for some beauty rest. We can catch it off guard at dawn when itÆs at rest, all we have to do is get close to it and kill it while itÆs still sleeping. Simple enough. WeÆll have to get up early though - letÆs meet at the bar at 3 in the morning.
Carefulģ We gotta be real quiet. We canÆt let it hear us.
WeÆre there. ItÆs close, I can sense it. If you see it, aim for the head. DonÆt get too close, though. Chimeras can smell you a mile off, even when theyÆre sleeping.
Nicely done! You should look into hunting chimeras as a profession.
ItÆs over. Our job is done. LetÆs head back. Remember to pop in to see Trapper at Yanov and tell him the wounded chimera is done with to get your share.
IÆve asked everywhere but itÆs useless. Nobody knows a thing, itÆs like heÆs a poltergeist or something.
Talk to the boss, IÆm more into shooting than beating gums.
IÆve received a message from the recon unit.
ģNoģ What the hell is that?!ģ Wait!!! WeÆre all dead!!!ģ Arghhh!ģ Help!ģ Someone help me!ģ Get that thing away from me!!!ģ Nooooo!
Recon unit, come in! What is going on out there?
We need to find the recon unit. Major, find out what happenedģ What the hellģ hrmģ You be careful out there, OK? And Major, keep me posted.
IÆve found the unit ¢ theyÆre all dead. Looks like they were shot, but thereÆs no trace of the enemy.
ItÆs those damn fanatics! We need to deal with them.
ItÆs those damn fanatics! They donÆt let upģ We're also missing a sentry, heÆs not answering and thereÆs not a trace of him eitherģ WeÆve got to take care of those bastards once and for all!
The recon unit had information about the location of Monolith forces. The notes indicate the book store.
Very well, IÆll be sending two boys your way. They'll meet you near the target. Over and out.

Very well, IÆll be sending two boys your way. They'll meet you near the targetģ And another thing, weÆve just picked up the sentryÆs signal. The coordinates place him somewhere near the grocery store. Check it out if you canģ IÆm counting on you, Major. Over and out.
I told you to put your shooter away! IÆm gonna count to three and if you donÆt do it IÆll blow your friggin' head off!
There, thatÆs betterģ now you can come on over and weÆll talk.

Major, I have a favor to ask. Our sentry isnÆt respondingģ You know where the grocery store used to be? ThatÆs where the signal is coming from. Investigate it if you canģ One other thing: be careful. Over and out.
Major, Kovalsky here. WeÆve got another emergency on our hands. Get over here and IÆll fill you in on the details.
Base, the sentry is dead. He was acting strange and attacked me. I also identified and eliminated a controller in that area.

Copy that, Major. The controller must have taken over the sentryģ He was a good guyģ That must have been what happened to the recon unit ¢ the controller forced them to attack each otherģ Anywayģ Over and out.
Base, the sentry is dead. He was acting strange and attacked me. I also found a controller in that area, so I had to fall back.
Copy that, Major. The controller must have taken over the sentryģ You need to kill that asshole right now! WeÆre at great risk as long as itÆs still alive.
Base, the controller has been eliminated.
Major, I need to talk to youģ privately.

Major, weÆve got a serious problemģ This operation has been getting more ridiculous every minute since the helicopters crashed. The men are dropping like flies, weÆve got no comms with HQ and the situation just keeps getting worseģ The evacuation choppers didnÆt show up again, and at this rate it wonÆt be long before we lose control of the situation completely. We need to establish communications with HQ right now!

Our communications expert believes that the signal is being jammed from somewhere close to us, heÆs working on getting the coordinates as we speak. I sent some of my men to check out a possible source of the interference, but they went missing and their personal signals arenÆt registering.

Major, one of your objectives is to establish communications with HQ. Given the situation we find ourselves in, this should be your priority from now onģ Also, as a personal favor to me, please find my boys.
Base, I found one of your men. It looks like he blew himself up with a grenade. There are no signs of a firefight or any other evidence of him being attacked.
Kovalsky here. Roger that, Majorģ Hey, IÆve got good news: weÆve finally acquired the exact coordinates of the source of interference. The signal is coming from the kindergarten building.
Get out of here you bastards or IÆll take you all out!!!
This crap wonÆt save you when the muties crawl out of the woodworkģ Here, take this and get out of my face.
Hah, you think thatÆll protect you from an emission? Here, take this and run, save your skin.
Argh!.. This is it, this is the end!!!ģ ItÆs here!! Mutants! Mutants everywhere!!ģ Argh!!!

Recording 12. My research will be over soonģ People at the camp are starting to suspect something. Grouse and Danila are planning to hunt some bloodsuckers ģ I wonder when theyÆll figure it outģ I need to sort the jars of blood to find out which ones have been contaminated, then hide the restģ SomeoneÆs coming. . Recording 13ģ possibly my last. First that nosy hunter and now Grouseģ I wonÆt get away with this. TheyÆll come for me, I know it. DoesnÆt really matter ¢ this hemoglobin dependency will kill me soon anyway. And IÆd rather die from a bullet than a diseaseģ
I knew IÆd be exposed eventuallyģ IÆm sure you have many questionsģ
Hey, what are you doing here?! Go back to where you came from while youÆve still got legs!
One more step and heÆs dead!
Why, is that change I still hear jingling in your pockets? Well, is it?
Run, we can still make it!
IÆll meet you by the old train station.
Hey, where are you going? Wait, come back here!!
I still think that we should sell the artifact and use the money to recruit people. This way we can attack the bandits together.
No wayģ WeÆre much better off giving them the artifact as ransom.
You donÆt think theyÆll shoot him as soon as they have the artifact?
They'll definitely shoot him if we come out blasting!
We canÆt give in to them! No matter what you give them, they always want more!
WeÆre not giving in. Complying with their conditions is the only option we have!
Well, that clears things up. Take them down.
OK, itÆs a deal. WeÆll go together. Whatever fighters you see, send æem my way and IÆll make a team out of them. Once everything is ready, give me a shout. WeÆll pick up Nitro and head for Jupiter.
One down, but there were two of them, so watch out!
That should be it, that was the last one.

OK, IÆll tell you how to kill controllersģ The other day I chanced upon a buttload of artifacts and decided to stash them while still en route, æcause my back couldnÆt hack it anymore. On the way back I got to my stash and there it was: a controller, chilling right on top of it. Now I thought to myself: ōcontroller or no controller, I gotta get my shit back!ö Then I realized that if I shield myself with something, its manipulation bullshit wonÆt work on me. So I picked up a metal grate that I found lying around and started sneaking up on the beast. By the time it woke up to what was going on, I'd pumped half a clip into the cocksucker's head. Just as it started trying to control me, I gave it a final bash with my rifle butt and that was that.

You heard about the Oasis? Of course youÆve heard of it. I, on the other hand, have actually been there. I was the first person to work out a way to get there. A pioneer, if you will. There ainÆt no mutants or frigginÆ anomalies there, itÆs quiet as a graveyard and full of artifacts. I was there only a few days agoģ sunbathing and all thatģ Lead you there? Hah! Sorry, but I donÆt want it getting crowded. Nothing personal.

IÆll tell you everything there is to say about bloodsuckersģ When I was at Zaton, those assholes made life real difficult for just about everyone. Stalkers kept at æem day and night, but it didnÆt help one bitģ poor bastards. One day I decided to help them out, so I took my shotgun and went straight for the bloodsucker lair. Three days I was partying with those sons of bitches, at times taking on two or even three together. He-heģ After some quality time with yours truly the bloodsucker problem at Zaton is all but over¢ the bastards keep their tentacles tucked in at all times.

Well, IÆm sure you know about the path from Zatonģ No? Well, the guide used to take a massive detour. What I did was create a short cut by cutting across a straight line. A novel idea. I tried a few places and worked out the way through the anomalies pretty quick. So I sold that info to the guide and now he takes everyone that way.

Well, IÆve got a lot of stories to tellģ Did I tell you about my time working for the scientists? So, one day I go and pop into their bunker, real curious to see what theyÆre up to in there. At first theyÆre all in shock, but then they go: ōyouære exactly what we need right now.ö It turns out they wanted to set up their devices under anomalies. He-heģ Obviously the eggheads didnÆt feel like doing it themselves, so they promised me cash and other goodies. So I was like: ōwell, thatÆs fine but the goodies better be worth it.ö I had to crawl around that area looking for anomalies, but it wasnÆt half as hard as getting those nerds to fork up the reward money.

You should have seen me getting this one artifact the other day ¢ that was something. You know anything about the quarry? Well, whatever decent shit there once was is long gone. ThereÆs no point looking in anomalies Flint has looked in, everyone knows that.

There might be a few folk who think that Flint is telling liesģ Well, they can ask anyone at the base about meģ Even this squad of pro hunters, when they spotted me the other day they were all like: ōWe need you, Flint, we canÆt do it without you!öģ They were hunting a chimera, so I had to show these ōprosö how itÆs done. My MO is real frigginÆ simple ¢ I just head straight into the lair. They left one rookie by the entrance to keep an eye out for it. Of course, come morning, soon as the newbie saw the beast he shat his pants and legged it, so I had to deal with it myselfģ Successfully, as you can see. He-heģ
Hah, now thatÆs some story, Flintģ
FlintÆs a real man, that's for sure.

Looks like the crew died in mid-air and the helicopter simply crashed because there was nobody there to fly it. The onboard computer was a little luckier. I wonder if thereÆs anything useful hereģ Ah, there's something Ś a map of the area with certain locations marked. That should come in handy.
So? WeÆve been waiting for you for a long time nowģ
WeÆll give it to æem good!
Take a chill pill, man ¢ weÆll take them easy!
What theģ?! Did they hide or something?
You idiots brought a tail with you!!!
Look at the claw marksģ strangeģ
The bodies we found before didnÆt have thoseģ
The hunter isnÆt hereģ LetÆs go.
Are you dumb?! Get back before I blow your brains out!!
Soģ The underpass was filled with gas after it was sealedģ
The entrance to the underpass is hereģ If we activate the generator, we'll be able to open the transportation lock.
Schematics for the Prypiat 1 underpassģ This is proof that there is an underground way to get to Prypiat.
All the electronics inside burned out, even the power cable sheaths meltedģ
The electronics are the same here, but the emission protection system is intactģ What could have caused this?
Well, thatÆs thatģ It was sweet, watching you work.
Well, thatÆs thatģ What the hell were you doing? Admiring the scenery?
We got the bastards! Thanks for your help.
We got the bastards! You're not keen to get involved in a real fight, are you?
Execute them, I donÆt need witnesses!
WeÆll wait here. The clients should show up soon.
Hey man, time is money. WeÆre done waiting for you, the squad is going back now.
So youÆre the hole expert, eh? Ha-haģ DonÆt worry, you can count on the boys to take the muties out nice and quick.
HmmģIt looks quiet. We can advance.
Looks like that was the last of them.
WeÆll wait for you here. If anything happens, we got your back.
It's all goodģ there were a few hamsters in here but we dealt with themģ
Watģ watchģ outģ contrģ controlģ controllģ erģ arghģ
Listen up, Sultan wants to see youģ
The job is done ¢ hereÆs the artifact.
JobÆs done. Here, take the artifact.
Wow, thatÆs a nice shooter ¢ let me have a look at it.
ThatÆs that dude with the nice shooter. Hey, wait up!
Good, now get the hell outta here before I change my mind!
YouÆre still here? I'm not a patient man, you know.
OK, thatÆs it. YouÆre going down!
Bros, he doesnÆt want to cooperate. Drop him!
Bros, this asshole doesnÆt want to share!

Two bodiesģ Looks like they died in the crash. Everything of value has been looted and the onboard computer has been wiped cleanģ But the black box is still intactģ interesting. I reckon a decent technician will be able to extract the information on it and decode itģ
ģStingray 4, come in. Come in, damn it! Stingray 4, do you read?ģ Attention Stingray group, this is Stingray 1. The emergency evacuation position is B28, I repeat ¢ B28.
Oh crap, we better get outta here, pronto.
Hmmģ holyģ what theģ What isģ whatģ againģ
Arghģ impossiģ bleģ Terribleģ Man, my head is killing meģ

Information on the onboard computerģ OK, so Stingray 3 had engine failure in both enginesģ Hrmģ What else is thereģ A map with what look like possible evacuation locations. Positions B2, B28 and B205. Interestingģ
This is Owlģ Come see me ¢ we need to talk about our business.
The jobÆs done. You can collect the goods.
Hello, stalker. Thinking of placing an order?
WhatÆs up? Decided to order something?
ThatÆs your order complete. Collect your goods.
I have the goods! Take them.
Take your goods, stalker! As you can see, I keep my word.
DonÆt worry, youÆll get the goods a bit later.
Come back later ¢ I donÆt have the goods yet.
Drop by later, IÆll have your order by then!
Sultan, I dealt with that loser real good! ThatÆll teach him not to mess with us!
Sultan sends his regards. Open wide, punk!
Hold onģ Find himģ The worst is yet to coģ Argh.
Alpha, beta and gamma radiation readings taken!
Acoustic data acquired for all ranges!
Hey, are you covering us or what?
Stalker!ģ What the hell are you doing?
TheyÆre attacking us!ģ Stalker!ģ What theģ?
We're done here, stalker. Now letÆs get the hell outta hereģ
OK, letÆs do this fast. The quicker we finish, the more chance we have of staying alive.
Looks like a quiet villageģ Unless we disturb the local residentsģ
So peacefulģ ItÆs like a resort.
Gavrilenko, I found out about the warehouse! ItÆs Freedom and there should be lots of them. Bring your whole squad and weÆll deal with those punks real fast!
Kremen, take your men and join Gaiduk at the warehouse. Those Duty bastards should come for their stuff any minute now.
If this checks out, youÆll get access to the warehouse.
Great, now the boys will be able to fight them offģ I donÆt forget favors, so you can use the stash any time you want. IÆll let everyone know.
Attention Freedom, this is Duty territory! Get the hell away from the warehouse!
Sure thing, just give us a minute to stock up on some ammoģ
Bastards!ģ Well, they asked for itģ Men, commence with the operation!
This ainÆt no public park, boy. You best be on your way.
Hey, get out of here! Duty members only!
Lieutenant Kirillov, Kovalsky here. I need you to escort the Major.
Major, the mercenaries are in position.
IÆm guessing the one by the main entrance is their leader.
Look, thereÆs another group. LetÆs see which one of them is the messengerģ
The targets are by the main entrance.
TheyÆre done, time to take them down.
Both targets escaped. Abort mission and fall back.
Both targets eliminated. Well done. Mission accomplished. Fall back.
ōģfind a way out! Help! We're running low onģ"
ōģmuch longer! WeÆve been without water for two days! Requesting a rescue mission! Helģö
ōģrequire immediate assistance! We wonÆt hold out for more than threeģö

ģWhichever way we go, we always end up at the same exact spot. I still canÆt get used to this fogģ ItÆs weird that Chepurny disappeared without a trace. Maybe he found a way out?ģ Day sixģ We have run out of waterģ The Colonel flipped his lid, shot two people at point blank range and then himselfģ Now IÆm on my own in this damn anomalyģ If I hadnÆt fixed for our communications with HQ to go down, they could have rescued usģ and Duty wouldnÆt have existed. I wonder how things would have turned outģ Ah, screw it. That Captain Tachenko died a long time agoģ And this General Tachenko of Duty will follow himģ that's justice, I supposeģ
Acoustic data collected for two ranges so far.
WeÆre getting there. The torsion field scan is almost done.
See, you can do it, when you want toģ Time to take the oath.
Do you swear to serve Duty with courage and integrity, to protect humanity from the ZoneÆs aggression and always follow the Code?
What are you standing around for? Have a seat and letÆs talk.
We donÆt have a strict hierarchy. Earn our respect and you get plenty of followersģ However, when on a mission we obey the commander's orders without question.

We respect the freedom of our members and other stalkers and we believe that the Zone is open to all, being the greatest gift humanity has ever received. ItÆs an opportunity to take a tremendous leap in our development and everyone needs to understand thatģ
Stalker, could you come over here?
We can sit around here all day but that wonÆt get us anywhere.
There's nothing but mutants around here.
The first thing we need to do is work out where the hell we are.
Great, thatÆs just what we need.
Ohģ, ehģ itÆs youģ Come inģ
Whatģ what can I do you for? Hah!
HereÆs to a good dayÆs work.
What?!ģ Sixģ Sixty two?ģ ThatÆs impossible!
ģIÆm telling you thatÆs not enough juiceģ Not enoughģ
ģHey guys, where are you goingģ Nah, IÆllģ IÆll stay hereģ
ItÆs over thereģ help yourself to a drinkģ .

Cardan, that old drunk. Not only is he a lazy bastard once heÆs had a drink - ōitÆs better here, we need to take it easyö ¢ but then he goes and takes a run at me and Joker, saying weÆre young and ainÆt got a clueģ Hehģ JokerÆs a piece of work too: gets pissed off, grabs his bag and disappears. While I was trying to calm Cardan down he left and hell if I know where he went. And what I am supposed to do? Drink with that senile moron? No thank you! His vodka is shitģ Ewwģ And to think how promising things were at the beginningģ
You thought IÆd be easy, huh?
Get up against a wall, these bastards are coming from all sides!
Here we are. Yanov station is a haven of relative safety in the area around Jupiterģ This is where we go our separate ways, but if you need me look for me by the bar.

First time here?ģ IÆm relatively new here myself. The nameÆs Nitro ¢ I work with equipmentģ I can repair anything you need, but getting some good tools is a problem around here. If you see anything, bring it straight to me. And if you need any technical help, come see me and IÆll be glad to do what I can for you.
Is that really you? Well, IÆll be! We were starting to think weÆd never see our doc again!ģ
Well, itÆs a long storyģ IÆll tell you another timeģ
Thanks for your helpģ By the way, the Colonel wants you to pay him a visit.
Also, Vano asked me to tell you that he and Garry headed for Yanov station. He said theyÆll bring back souvenirsģ
Also, Garry headed for Yanov station to restock. He asked me to tell you that he wonÆt be back for a few days.
So, tell me, where were you?
YouÆre not going to believe me anyway, butģ In a fridgeģ
Wow, never heard that one beforeģ A fridge, ehģ
What fridge? Where did you find it?
Where did I find itģ IÆd like to see you find anything in my state.
And what state would that be?
Hrmģ IÆm not quite sure how to describe itģ
Congratulations on joining Duty!ģ Follow me! Left, right, left, right!
Turn back, stalker. This place ainÆt for you.
Stalker, off with you while youÆve still got legs.

So, thatÆs where those damn mutants are!ģ They climbed up to the plateau thinking that Noah wonÆt get to themģ I guess they donÆt know that IÆve got a personal path up there by the northern farmsteadģ YouÆre in for a fun ride, assholes!!
Hey, itÆs Trapper. I've just had a hunter here who saw a chimera last night. Judging by the description, itÆs the same one weÆre after. Come see me if you canģ ItÆs important.
Hey, stalkerģ CÆmere, IÆve got a question for you.
Come back, broÆ. We donÆt need a scout on this mission.
DonÆt rush ahead, itÆs best to stick together around here.
Hey man, where are you going? LetÆs work together on this one!
Keep up, broÆ ¢ weÆve gotta keep moving forward.
Hey, so are we moving or what?
This isnÆt the best place to twiddle your thumbs. LetÆs go.
Hey man, quit wasting time. Do it long enough and weÆll get wasted ourselves!
LetÆs talk once this is over.
Quietģ keep it nice and quietģ
WeÆll talk once we get out of here.
This is hardly the time to talkģ
Iģ I donÆt feel like talking.
Listen, this isnÆt the best time to chat.
IÆll be honest with you ¢ this is neither the time nor the place to talk.
What the hell are you doing?
Hey, I donÆt stand for that kind of shit!
I suggest you stop that right now.
Well, at least the protective suits will get some use.
LetÆs see if thereÆs a way of getting through this.
Come, IÆve found a way through.
Hrm, looks like the direct route is not an option.
No worries, real men often take the backdoor.
Hrmģ something feels wrong here tooģ
Well, broÆģ letÆs rock and roll.
Mess with the best, die like the rest!
Damn, I knew we shouldnÆt have come here.
We're out in the open hereģ letÆs move.
Hrm, go this way once and it may be your last.
OK, thereÆs a way through hereģ letÆs hope it leads somewhere a little less deadly.
The gate is blocked and thereÆs no button here. Anyone feel like going to the control room and seeing if thereÆs anything there to help us?
ItÆs blocked and thereÆs no button. I get the feeling someoneÆs gonna have to make for the control room to solve this one.
So, the gate is blocked, huh? Hrm, I donÆt knowģ Maybe it can be opened from the control room?
The gate is blocked. If I were you, IÆd have a look around the control room.
Muzzle flash on the right! Err, I mean left!
IÆm getting a strange feelingģ And not the good kind.
Put your weapons on the ground, now!
DonÆt shoot! IÆm Lieutenant Sokolov, copilot of Stingray 4. IÆm on my way to B28, the emergency position.
I have information for Kovalsky. Take me to him.
Who are you?! WhatÆs your business with the commander?!
IÆm Major Degtyarev, USS. IÆm here to investigate the failure of Operation Fairway.
Stalkers helped me get to Prypiat.
YouÆre a merry gang, ainÆt you?
What did you think, weÆre here for fun? Why so trigger-happy? CanÆt you see that weÆre all friends here? Me and agentģ have been through a lot. Trust me on that!
I have no business with the armyģ
And I donÆt give a ratÆs ass whoÆs working for who.
I have a bad feeling about thisģ

I gotta get away from these assholes, the sooner the better. The loot IÆve stashed behind the substation should last me a whileģ ItÆs time to move onģ IÆm sure theyÆre already looking for meģ ThatÆs alright, though. WeÆll see who gets who in the endģ
The bastard is sleepingģ Crab says helloģ Fire!

I spoke to the HQ commander and reported the situation to him along with all the information thatÆs been collectedģ There wonÆt be any helicopter support for a while. He suggested that we fortify our position and await further ordersģ USS observers are currently at the HQ, so youÆll be able to talk to your superiors soon enough.
Colonel, a radio signal has been detected and its source is not far from the base! The transmission is encrypted and I havenÆt been able to crack it yet.
Yes, I have the coordinatesģ but the signal is intermittent andģ itÆs movingģ
The signal is back! Transmitting coordinatesģ
ItÆs back!ģ ItÆs right next to you!ģ
ItÆs close and itÆs moving toward our base!ģ
Major, Kovalsky here. You better get back here ¢ we need to prepare for a possible attack. Bastards wonÆt catch us with our pants down this time!
DonÆt shoot! IÆm headed to the main entrance.
YouÆre the stalker who disabled the Scorcher?
Yesģ But my plan didnÆt work. I came here to speak with your commanders. I have information that will allow the army to take control of the Zone and destroy it.
WeÆve got a problemģ Our choppers are crashing for reasons that weÆve not understood yet.
Until we work it out, there wonÆt be any helicopter supportģ The only way to get to the borders of the Zone is on footģ
The reasons are obvious ¢ there are many anomalies in the air, especially in the center of the Zoneģ
We had a map with a safe air corridor between anomalies! And the helicopters had emission protection systems!ģ

ThatÆs oddģ YouÆre telling me you haven't noticed that anomalies move around after an emissionģ Some disappear and new ones show up in different placesģ Which makes your map of anomalies effectively uselessģ
Emissions donÆt just follow one another. If you can scan the area and identify all the anomalies, the choppers will be able to collect us safely, provided theyÆre dispatched right after an emission.
We must inform the HQ of this as soon as possible.
IÆll contact my commanders right after the emission.
Lieutenant, get æem on the line as soon as you can.
Major, if you don't mind, could you holster that weaponģ
Listen, Major, holster your weapon, would you? We wonÆt shoot you, you have my word.
Come on, Major, put your gun away for GodÆs sake. EveryoneÆs jumpy enough as it is.
Stalker, we don't carry weapons around on the ship. So do me a favor and put yours away.
Skadovsk is a safe area. Holster your weapon.
Stalker, if you want to get on board, put that gun away.
Come on, man, put that shooter away. This is our camp, you donÆt need it here.
Hey man, this here place is as peaceful as it gets, so holster that shooter of yours and keep it that way.
Lookit, weapons are for killing mutants and you wonÆt find any in here, so put it away now, would ya?
Slow down, chiefģ Put that shooter away firstģ
Stalker, if you want to enter, holster your weapon.
Put that gun away ¢ this is a neutral area.
WeÆre in the firing line as it is, stalker. If you donÆt put that weapon away youÆre asking for trouble.
So long as thereÆs a gun in your hand, you ainÆt getting in, you got that?
Stalker, I must warn you that entering the bunker with a weapon is strictly prohibited.
Stalker, you must holster your weapon by the entrance to the research facility.
You hear me?ģ Holster your weapon.
All right people, itÆs time to head backģ YouÆre useless, so donÆt expect a reward.
You shouldnÆt have come here, stalkerģ Now youÆll be worm food.
What the hell are you doing? You shot me!
Heya, itÆs Nitro. Swing by for that memory module, itÆs ready for you.
Nice jump! Feel free to drop by sometime.
LetÆs talk afterģ my jaws jam up during emissions.
Another emissionģ IÆm getting real tired of themģ
Gahģ I feel like IÆm in a trench with tanks rolling over it.
Man, I thought acid rain was bad enough around hereģ
Forget about itģ IÆd trade it for rain any day of the week and twice on Sundayģ
Damn, that's loud! My ears are still ringingģ I feel like IÆm deaf!ģ
What? Haha, just messing with you!
Hehģ Every time thereÆs an emission I get this splitting headacheģ
As long as the emission ainÆt killing you, IÆd say thatÆs pretty good goingģ
It looks like the emission is over. Kirillov, contact HQ!
Colonel, why did comms go down?
Because of the emission. ItÆs impossible to establish comms during one.
OKģ All right, listen up. Operation Fairway is being taken over by the USS. Is Major Degtyarev there?
OK, heÆs in command from now onģ Tell him to stay put, heÆll be contacted by his superiors shortly.
Major Degtyarev here. I read you.
Good. As of this moment you are responsible for completing Operation Fairway. Kovalsky and the Stingray squad will assist you. Report.

I managed to acquire documents about experiments carried out in the Zone. Furthermore, weÆve been contacted by a stalker who has been in the CNPP. His name is Strelok and heÆs offered us his cooperation.
ģ Roger. Accept his offer. Do you know what caused our helicopters to crash?

Yes, I have the data and Strelok's information confirms it. The helicopters were hit by anomalies after their locations changed during the emission. The anomaly map issued to the pilots turned out to be useless.
Strelok believes that the helicopters ran into anomalies. The location of anomalies changes after every emission ¢ a fact that was missed when making the map for Operation Fairway.

Goodģ That means we have until the next emissionģ YouÆll be collected together with the stalker from position B28. The Stingray squad will be evacuated with you. The birds are already in the air. Over and out.
You heard the news, fellas. When we go, stick together and keep the stalker alive. Any questions? Get ready to move.
LetÆs get to the chopper and we'll talk in the air, OK?
WeÆll have plenty of time to talk later. We need to make sure we have enough ammo.
Well, this was a decent base ¢ the old girl kept us alive, canÆt ask for more than that. But now weÆve gotta get to the birds and fly home.
Major, can I have a word?
Major, we canÆt wait any longer.
Would you look at that ¢ the sons of bitches actually made it. You were right, Strelok.
LetÆs move to the right along the building, double time!
ThereÆs about a dozen zombies in the building.
Move to the left and cover each other.
Every zombie in Prypiat is going to know weÆre coming! WeÆve got to move faster. Run!
Our orders are to take off if we come under attack, so hurry the hell up.
Roger that, weÆll be there soon.
Stay alert and follow orders! WeÆre real close to the evacuation point. LetÆs move.
To the choppers, go go go!
Come on, boys, one last push!
Get in the chopper, I hope it was worth it.
Major Degtyarev, we're ready to go.
Whoa, so you decided to stay? Well, if you need anything swing by the base. IÆm back there now and this time IÆve got stalkers with me.
Shit! Strelok is downģ But we still have to get out of here. Go!
We can't wait any longer! WeÆre leaving in sixty seconds!
See, your suit can handle it, keep going.
Wow, I canÆt believe we made it to Prypiat unscathed. How did you even manage to find this pathģ
ItÆs not that difficult. All you need is skill and a bit of luck, and we got bothģ Or maybe itÆs the Zone rewarding us for our patience. God knows IÆve paid my dues with the scientists.
Yeahģ IÆve had enough of Hermann myselfģ
I signed up as soon as I found out you need help. IÆll follow you to the bitter end, man!
Take them out before they snap out of it!
This is all very strangeģ SomeoneÆs controlling these fanaticsģ Hrm, weÆll get back to this later.
This is all very strangeģ SomeoneÆs controlling these fanaticsģ Hrm, weÆll get back to this later. Our sentry hasnÆt showed up yet, so let me know if you find him. Over and out.
You know, I thought IÆd be safer with youģ
Holy shitģ this place is teeming with them.

Kovalsky, something strange is going on here. There were loads of Monolith fighters inside the building. They were all in a trance and talking toģ a pile of trash. I examined this pile and if you cut through the crap, it resembles a primitive antenna. It seems that someone talks to them through this antenna and they believe it to be divine interventionģ
O Monolith, we do not hear Youģ
Why have you left us, O Monolith?
We await your orders, O Monolithģ
IÆm by the building but all the entrances are blocked.
The squad sent to look for the source of interference had explosives. Use them to clear the entrance.
Man, they donÆt let up, do they?!
Our Monolith comrade has been killed!
Hrm, the power to the gate has been cut.
Great, the gate has no power!
Looks like thereÆs no power being supplied to the gate.
ThereÆs been a power cut, haha.
This looks like a transformer. I wonder if all the lines have been activated.
A transformer. I remember these from my childhoodģ
Listen, there should be levers on this thing that you can turn stuff on withģ
Heyģ you be careful with that thing, all right?
Hey, donÆt go there, itÆs closed off!
Keep moving, broÆ. IÆve got your back.
Keep moving, buddy. IÆve got your rear covered, donÆt you worry.
IÆll wait down here and cover our backs, OK?
IÆll stay here and cover the approaches.
Well, the way is clear. LetÆs go?
The doors opened over there. Shall we go that way?
The door is open. Shall we go?
No point standing around here, thatÆs for sure.
My gut feeling is that weÆve made itģ
The recon unit only spotted Monolith patrols, they didnÆt see any large squads or unitsģ

I donÆt understand how they could have attacked so suddenlyģ without leaving any evidenceģ really strangeģ Major, weÆve just picked up the sentryÆs signal. The coordinates place him somewhere near the grocery store. Check it out if you canģ IÆm counting on you, Major. Over and out.
I donÆt understand how they could have attacked so suddenlyģ without leaving any evidenceģ really strangeģ I need to think things over. WeÆll speak again soon.
ThatÆs everyone, except for the stalker. HeÆs deadģ Prepare for takeoff.
ģģ the stalker and the Stingray squad are deadģ Prepare for takeoff.
Hello. What brings you to our humble vessel?
Welcome aboard our swamp icebreaker! Hah!
The ship has been ashore a long time, but itÆs still full of ratsģ To what do we owe this dubious pleasure?
Drop in if something interesting comes upģ Skadovsk wonÆt be going anywhere, thatÆs for sureģ
Pop in if you find any artifacts and weÆll see what we can doģ
Are we done here? Yes? Good. The exit is over there.
Yeah, you go on nowģ Dealing with leeches is not high on my list of prioritiesģ
What are you - a statue? If you got something - spill the beans, if not - take a hike.
ThatÆs right, get on outta here.
DonÆt let the door hit you.
WhatÆs up? You wounded or suffering from radiation sickness?
Hello. ItÆs always nice to see a new face in our bunker.
Ehmģ very well, come on over. What can I do for you?
Yes, you have my undivided attentionģ
So, you managed to drag your ass over here? Bravo.
See youģ if you manage to stay in one piece, that isģ
Drop by some time, weÆll have a good time.
Come on over, broÆ. I got all the shit youÆre ever gonna need.
Pop in some time, it can be fun around here.
YouÆre a touch pale, buddy. How about a little injection or two?
Wounded again? You donÆt waste any time, do you?
Nextģ Err, I mean, take care and stay healthy.
See you again some time, stalker.
OK, thatÆs us. Have a good one.
Come on in, man. Relax, take a load off.
Well, I propose a toast to a brave squad of Duty men, sent to the frontlines for some serious green scum asskicking!

Have I told you the story about Flint? First he set up stalker hunters to get attacked by a chimera, then he left his partner to die in an anomaly and thenģ he joined Freedom. IÆd say that a pretty logical conclusion right there. So, I say we drink to the hope that all members of Freedom follow his lead, and weÆll take care of whoeverÆs left!
LetÆs drink to the fact that even in the center of the Zone there are people who are willing to serve Duty. To our new squad!
I raise my glass to the fact that this research complex is now fully protected by Duty from mutants, anarchists and other scum.
Fellas, letÆs drink to honor our brave volunteers, who are taking readings in some of the ZoneÆs most dangerous areas for the good of our scientific progress!
I propose a toast to Duty fighters, who courageously fought off a cowardly attack made by those filthy punks!

Boys, have you heard how Duty is sticking to its traditions? Take Warrant Officer Morgan. In following the strict need to maintain his factionÆs reputation, he felt it was his duty to sell off half his warehouse on the side!

Fall in! Remember: DutyÆs stalwart traditions were laid down by General Tachenko himself. He lied to his comrades, misled the HQ and ōbravelyö died in a space bubble. HereÆs to all members of Duty following his lead!

You know, the first time I saw our new squad, I asked them: ōFellas, would you prefer to join Duty instead? YouÆre free to do as you like, after allö. You know what they said to me? ōNo, thank you. With them itÆs all about orders ¢ to kill, primarily. No wonder their eyes are emptyģ We've had enough of zombiesģö

A member of Duty came up to one of our people guarding the bunker and asked him: ōProfessor, could you tell me which artifact is best for fighting boars, Wrenched to protect you from radiation or Eye to help you with wounds?ö You know what the answer was? ōShell is what you need, son. If you see a boar, run back to your base as fast as you can!ö Hah!

People, you know how the scientists came to choose our boys to take anomaly measurements? They actually went to see Shulga first and said to him: ōThereÆs a job that needs doing for the benefit of science.ö And he said: ōNo problem, who do I have to execute?ö

With every day that goes by the need for Duty becomes clearer to me. I mean, who else would put together a whole warehouse of weapons and ammo and then donate it to Freedom? It was really nice of them, wouldn't you say?
HeÆs dead already. And if it were up to me IÆd have all of yous follow suitģ Teach you to quit exporting the ZoneÆs products outside, driven by that same greed.
Nobody knows what really happened. ItÆs not like you were there to see it.

Is that the squad made up of Monolith people? Well, I hope you find yourself a bloodsucker squad too, æcause once you do the Lieutenant Colonel will definitely add you to the list of dangerous mutants.
Hah, some guards you are. Just last year the mercenaries had you running around the Dark Valley with your tail between your legs. Or have you forgotten?
Yeah, I can imagine the readings the scientists are getting. Air samples: 100% smoke, water samples: 100% vodka, and idiotic laughter on all sound frequencies.

ThatÆs alright, weÆll get our weapons back. You guys have developed a nifty habit of dropping weapons and artifacts whenever you run into our fighters. I donÆt blame you either ¢ canÆt be easy hauling ass, what with your pants shat and allģ
Yeah? Well, I seem to remember how your ōbrave squadö was on their knees, groveling for access to Yanov, before an emissionģ
EveryoneÆs got one of those, but DutyÆs a real haven for hypocrites. ōWeÆll protect the world from the Zone, right after we collect a few artifactsģö
Good deal, fanatics should stick with fanatics. You should wear Monolith uniforms too, lest stalkers might actually confuse you for humans.
From mutants? You sure youÆll have enough space in that bunker when the mutants come out in force? ItÆs not like itÆs the 100 Rads bar! You canÆt just hole up there and wait it out!
If your volunteers drink as much as you do, I can only imagine the readings the scientists are getting!
Yeah, thatÆs right. Bring in as many guards as you can. Now half the Zone knows about your equipment warehouse, so expect visitors!
Attention stalkers! An emission is approaching! I repeat, an emission is approaching! Seek cover immediately.
Attention stalkers! An emission will start any minute now! Find cover if you want to live!
Stalkers, the emission is finally over. I hope youÆre all in one pieceģ
Attention, attention! An emission is approaching, find cover immediatelyģ
Attention, attention! An emission is about to hit, find cover immediately!
The emission is over. I hope everyoneÆs OK. If you're hurt, come to Yanov and weÆll patch you up.
Attention all units, this is Colonel Kovalsky. According to our readings, an emission is approaching. Take cover immediately.
Attention all units, this is Colonel Kovalsky. An emission is starting! I repeat, an emission is starting! Take cover immediately.
Attention all units, this is Colonel Kovalsky. The emission is over.
Attention stalkers! Skadovsk is under attack. WeÆve been forced to batten down the hatches until things quiet down.
Attention stalkers! The doors of Skadovsk are open to you once more.
Attention all stalkers and anyone else who cares: Yanov station has been attacked. WeÆve blocked all the entrances for the time being.
Attention all stalkers and anyone else who cares: Yanov is secure again. YouÆre welcome to visit any time.
Attention, attention: This is Hermann. The science laboratory is under attack. We wonÆt be allowing stalkers in for a while.
Attention, attention: This is Hermann. Our mobile laboratory is open to stalkers once again.
Attention all units, this is Colonel Kovalsky. The base is under attack. The entrance has been blocked until the threat is eliminated.
Attention all units, this is Colonel Kovalsky. The threat has been eliminated.

IÆm positive the artifact is on the tree itself. ItÆs clear from here that I could climb onto the tree from the hill. I just hope the hills are free of mutants. ItÆs too bad I had to come here on my own after that argument with Cardan and Barge, but when I bring back the artifact theyÆll see I was right all alongģ
Major Degtyarev, weÆre taking heavy fire from an unidentified enemy. We cannot hold out for long.
Shit! ItÆs Monolith fighters. Fire at will and keep moving towards the choppers!
Those bastards again! Maybe we should go around the building?
ThereÆs no time. Check your weapons and fire at will!
Major, where are you? We need to get Strelok to the evacuation point!
Major, we canÆt wait for you much longer!
Major, we're going to have to keep moving!
So, how about pressing the button?
Lookit, press the button or something, yeah?
Why did I even get into this?ģ
The hell with this place! I canÆt wait till IÆm outta hereģ
I canÆt believe the shit they told meģ ōDonÆt worry, piloting is easy as pieöģ My assģ
You know, itÆs not so bad hereģ I think.
With loyal friends by your side, even this shit ain't that bad.
ItÆs a good thing I decided to follow youģ How else would I have seen a place like this?
I think I may have been here at some pointģ IÆm not sureģ
Strangeģ Everything about this place is strange.
What a mess! YouÆd get an asskicking for this kind of thing at our baseģ
This place is no sandy beach, thatÆs for sureģ
Goģ get out of here while you can.
Major, speak to the Colonel if you need something.
You must be looking for the Colonel, Major.
Colonel Kovalsky is the person to speak to, Sir!
Kovalsky is the man to speak to, Sir.
Sir, you should see Kovalsky if you need something.
I don't feel like beating gumsģ Talk to the commander.
The commander is the one to speak to if something comes up.
You'd be better off speaking to Strider.
Strider is the one you ought to talk to.

The documents refer to a central laboratory designated X8, which could contain information about secret experiments conducted in the Zone. Our analysts have been interested in this issue for quite some timeģ
Quit screwing around. Holster your weapon.
Come on, man, put that weapon away.
This ainÆt the time to yap, man.
Are you crazy? WeÆll talk after.
Sure you wanna chitchat with all this shit going on?
Put that shooter away and then weÆll talk.
IÆm not real talkative when thereÆs a gun being waved in my face. Shocking, eh?
I donÆt feel like talking when youÆve got me in your sights.
I donÆt feel like chatting with you.
I ainÆt in the mood for gum beating.
Talk to the boss if you need something.
The boss is over there, go talk to him.
Get that thing outta my face.
You think youÆre hard or something? Holster that shooter!
What the hell is wrong with you?
You picked a good time to talk!
I ainÆt talking to you when you got that thing in my face.
Put that shooter away, then weÆll talk.
Holster that shooter or get lost!
Go find someone else to annoy, buddy.
I ainÆt feelinÆ talkative right now.
Speak to the big bad boss.
I don't get paid to listen to your shit ¢ the boss does.
If you need something, the boss is over there.
This is a bad time to yap!
We can chat when that weapon is not in my face.
Either put the shooter away or just go away.
We'll talk when you holster your weapon.
Nah, I don't feel like talking.
Sorry, but IÆm not in the mood to chat.
Speak to the squad commander if you want something.
Stalker, direct your questions to the commander.
DonÆt play the hero, keep that shooter holstered.
Peace out, man. Holster that cannon of yours.
ThereÆs like shooting and stuff, you know?
It's hardly the time to talk!
You planning to shoot or talk? Holster that weapon.
I ainÆt keen on talking to people who are pointing a gun at me.
WeÆll talk once that shooter is holstered.
This conversation ainÆt happening and thatÆs the end of it.
I canÆt be bothered to chat now.
Go talk to the boss, man.
I doubt I can tell you anything usefulģ go see the chief.
Leave me alone, man. Speak to the boss, heÆs over there.
WeÆll talk when the fight is over!
Not a good time to talk, man!
If you want to talk, holster your weapon!
I ainÆt talking with you pointing that thing at me!
Holster your weapon if you wanna talk.
I ainÆt got the time to talk.
The commander can answer your questions.
I do shooting, the commander does Q & A sessions.
Monolith members near the disabled antenna

Your enemy must feel humiliated after being killed by you ten times in a row and failing to kill you even once during this time! You are not cruel, though - you're just giving him a reason to keep practicing.
You're deadly! Killing two birds with one stone... You skewered them both with your Gauss rifle.
Your calm and calculating approach brings a swift death to the enemy, as you expertly hit two targets with two shots of your double-barreled shotgun in the space of a couple of seconds.
You obviously know what being a stalker is all about, which is why you're become the number one legendary stalker!
Cutting through an exoskeleton with a knife? It looks like youÆre the kind who likes to cut right down to the bone.
They thought they had you, but you showed them who's boss by killing five of them, despite knock'in on death's doorstep yourself!
What an adrenalin rush! You annihilated your enemy with a hail of bullets - 5 kills in five seconds! Sweet!
Wow, great aim! You got two for the price of 1 by shooting the enemy in the eye without dying.
Let them hide behind corners and lay their ambushes - that won't stop you. Hell, you can hit a target on the move without even aiming!
Let him run as fast as he can - your bullets will catch him either way.
You have slaughtered twenty enemies in a row without dying! Wow, what a bloodbath!
There may have been three of them and they may have had sniper rifles, but your skill put an end to them.
You don't need to fire a single round to outwit the barbarians.
Distance is not a problem for a real sniper! You can hit the bull's eye at distances over 120 meters.
Your enemy got you down, but you left him a nice surprise with that live grenade!
They killed your friends and they deserve your unleashed vengeance. Three enemy corpses for three dead friends - that's justice.
You are your friend's guardian angel. You saved him twice from an attack since he was carrying the artifact.
You kept your honor and your team's by putting some points on the board.
Do you really know where the next artifact will show up? Well, you picked it up in a matter of seconds when it appeared!
You got lucky there - you killed the enemy with the last bullet in your clip! Better them than you, that's for sure!
You killed eight enemies in a row. You've drawn a line in the sand - let's see if they're willing to cross it.
Nobody killed you because you're careful and cautious. You didn't die once during the game and scored over fifteen points.
You do your work without attracting attention by always using a silencer. Why let the enemy know the direction of death?
You destroyed your enemy by winning and scoring twice as many points as their leading scorer.
Your bold plan to instantly capture the artifact was successful and within the first three minutes of the game you scored for your team.
You annihilated the enemy without giving up a single artifact. ThatÆll show them what real team work is all about.
You scored highest in every area by getting the most kills, the most artifacts and dying the least! Keep it up!
Your blade skills are unrivaled! You gutted three enemies in twenty seconds, showing off your hand skills.
Every enemy has a weakness, and you found it shooting him in the eye with your sidearm.
The enemy's bullets are powerless against you. You walk through them without a scratch and moe the enemy down!
YouÆre willing to research the most recent developments in the Zone. You have advanced our understanding of the Zone by finding new anomalous formations previously unknown to science.
Stalkers think more of you now.\nIn appreciation of your efforts, stalkers have taken to leaving ammo in your personal box at Yaniv station.
You donÆt try to avoid dangerous mutants - you hunt them down and do it well. Good hunters are held in high esteem among stalkers.
Stalkers think more of you now.\nAs a sign of respect, stalkers have taken to leaving medical supplies in your personal box at Skadovsk.

You uncovered the secret behind stalker disappearances at Skadovsk: the guilty party werenÆt mutants after all, but a far more dangerous foe - man. Your successful pursuit of the truth has earned you the respect of stalkers.
Stalkers think more of you now.\nStalkers have asked Owl the trader to give you a discount. \nBeard is now offering you his best prices for artifacts.
You showed the bandits and their leader, Sultan, whoÆs boss, thus making you a popular man among stalkers. YouÆre always welcome at Skadovsk.
You have earned the respect of bandits.\nOwl the trader has given you a discount out of fear that he may be your next victim. \nAs agreed, Beard will now pay you a cut of his proceeds.
You helped Sultan outmaneuver the stalkers and take BeardÆs business under your control. You are now feared and respected at Zaton.
Stalkers decided to give MagpieÆs loot, which he had acquired through lies and deceit, to someone more worthy. They put his stuff in your personal box at Yaniv.
You brought the scumbag to justice. Magpie cheated and deceived many a stalker, and now his time has come.
Stalkers think more of you now.\nBandits will try to hunt you down whenever you venture out into the Zone.
You found every type of artifact known to Zone researchers. This earned you the respect of stalkers and the close attention of bandits.
Cardan can perform a unique modification, which will allow you to move quickly while wearing an exoskeleton, by installing hydraulic servomotor boosters.
Now Cardan has all the tools heÆll ever need. This will allow him to perform new and more complex upgrades.
Azot can perform a unique tactical helmet modification: an infrared scanner that allows for detection of nearby humans.
Azot has all the tools a Zone technician could dream of. He can now perform hi-tech modifications.
Your experience allows you to find rare artifacts more often.
There are virtually no accessible areas by the center of the Zone that you havenÆt been to yet. YouÆve been a guest of almost every local anomaly.
Your leadership qualities improve your teamÆs chances of survival.
You had a team job to do, so you put a great squad together and proved you're a leader.
Freedom, Duty, free stalkers and even bandits have come to recognize your negotiation skills, and think highly of you as a result.

You know how to solve a problem peacefully. Who needs brute force when simple words can resolve a dispute just as well? Nonetheless, you should watch out for people that shoot first and ask questions later.
YouÆve worked with the scientists for so long that you could rightfully be called a research assistant. Everyone in the scientistsÆ bunker considers you one of their own.

Your relations with Duty have improved. If you find yourself in a pickle, Duty fighters will try to help you.\nAzot, the technician who favors Duty, has offered you a discount. \nYour relations with Freedom have deteriorated. \Hawaiian, the trader who favors Freedom, will now charge you more for his goods.
You have provided plenty of help to Duty, and this has earned you the respect of Duty fighters and their friends. But keep in mind that not everyone in the Zone is a Duty fan.

Your relations with Freedom have improved. If you find yourself in a pickle, Freedom fighters will try to help you.\Hawaiian, the trader who favors Freedom, has offered you a discount. 
Your relations with Duty have deteriorated. 
Azot, the technician who favors Duty, will now charge you more for his services.
You have provided plenty of help to Freedom, and this has earned you the respect of Freedom fighters and their friends. But keep in mind that not everyone in the Zone is a Freedom fan.
Stalkers at Yaniv value a balance of power between Duty and Freedom, which is why both the technician and the trader have given you discounts now.
You didnÆt side with either faction and always acted as a free stalker. YouÆve not made any enemies, but you havenÆt got any new friends either.
Traders offer you more rare and expensive goods.
Although you know what itÆs like to be short of cash, youÆre not doing too badly at the moment. In fact, sometimes your wealth can be considerable, and traders value a client of your stature.
After getting the notes, Strelok found a new reason to live on and make it to his objective, whatever the cost.
Your foresight has served you well: on finding notes in Strelok's groupÆs stashes, you kept them until you found their rightful owner.

You managed to survive an emission 3 times without taking cover thanks to the anabiotic pills. The effects of this on your psyche are unclear, as you notice a strange silence inside your mind. It seems that you no longer hear a constant stream of thoughts.
Owl the trader has offered you a discount for being his best informer and partner.
When you get your hands on a piece of info, you always find the right buyer. Obviously, you always have your own profit in mind when selling information to those who need it.
Your relations with stalkers have improved. If you find yourself in a pickle, stalkers will try to help you.\nStalker medics have agreed to sell you medical supplies at cost price.
YouÆve always helped out stalkers in trouble. Although their faction is not very tight-knit, you now have allies in the unofficial stalker brotherhood who will help you in combat and afterwards too.
Various new information allowed Professors Hermann and Ozersky to create a new experimental drug, an anabiotic, in the Zone.
You have a chance of surviving an emission even without the aid of drugs, provided your health is in order.

Given how useful you are, Professor Hermann is now offering you medical supplies and equipment intended exclusively for bunker personnel at cost price.\nNovikov is so used to you that he only wants you to pay for parts when performing modifications.
Find legendary Heart of the Oasis.
You killed 300 mutants Ś the Zone is a safer place, at least for a while.
You drank 100 energy drinks. The Zone is too big to be slow.
Find at least one artifact with the help of each detector.
Save all stalkers from Kovalsky group during the final evacuation.
Find and acquire items from every stash in Zaton which contains any weapon.
Enter Skadovsk for the first time.
Enter Yaniv for the first time.
Gather the squad and go to overpass.
Enter Laundromat for the first time.
Get all documents in Lab X-8.
Complete the game by evacuating on a helicopter.
Complete the game and decide to stay in the Zone.
Meet Petruha and help him get out of the anomaly.
Simultaneously equip Snowflake, Flame, Stone flower and Moonlight.
Finish the game on Novice difficulty.
Finish the game on Stalker difficulty.
Finish the game on Veteran or Master difficulty.
Listen, I have a question for you...
What do you think about Nimble?
Nimble? I think that one of these days we'll jump that slippery jackass in a quiet place!
You hear anything about the Oasis?

Well, I heard a funny story once. Basically, the boys at Garbage started a rumor about where the Oasis is, and made it real believable, too. Then, they went to that location and waited. Well, what can I say? So many gullible losers showed up to share their stuff that the boys who started the whole thing left the Zone soon afterwards. And why not? Fellas were set for life. Only one of them stayed behind, some guy called Yoga...
That, if you go there all your problems will be solved... I reckon that's gotta be handy - no more hangovers or hemorrhoids has gotta be good! Find it, set up a resort and let the cash pile up.

Why would I bother listening to that crap? All it is, is cheap talk for losers... Real men don't believe that shit. You know, you're sitting in a bar with a cold one in your hand and the stalkers are buzzing all around, arguing over where the Oasis is. And every one of those suckers swears he knows the location... Though for some reason it differs from sucker to sucker.
Ever meet a stalker called Magpie?

Sure have... One look at him told me he was a complete sucker and then I heard stalkers saying he's total scum, too. I don't know where he is though... You'll have to find him yourself if you're after him.
Nah... First time I've heard that name, buddy.
Do you know who could have stolen things from a personal box?
A rat. I reckon you'd better ask the bosses over at Yaniv, 'cause they're known for hunting a rat down.
Who could help me with decrypting something here?
Well, I heard Nitro at Yaniv is good. Apparently, back when he wasn't working for Duty the boys got him to crack stalker PDAs a couple of times... Ah, the good old days...

Don't get all scientific on me, chief. You're gonna need a hacker for that shit. Back when I was doing time I met one in the can but that's the only one I know. When I got out he still had about a fiver left...
I have the Duty trader's PDA. You know anyone who would be interested in it?
There's a trader at Skadovsk called Owl. He pays pretty good and the boys sell him stalker PDAs now and again. He doesn't ask too many questions either, so it's all good.

Just about anyone, buddy. Think about it: his own faction will bury the cocksucker alive, other factions will kick up a fuss, but Owl at Zaton would make that PDA disappear real good. Only thing is, only Owl knows where that PDA will turn up later on... It's up to you.
Where can I find tools around here?

Well, if you need surgical tools I'd look in hospitals and emergency stations. If you're looking for construction tools, then check by the quarry at Jupiter. If it's small tools you're after, then search old workshops.
How the hell should I know? I don't need tools to convince stalkers to cough up, just so you know... Relax, I'm just kidding.
Who can unblock a memory module?
Good question! I mean that's what I specialize in, right? Hah! Are you dumb, buddy? It's either gonna be technicians or scientists... You don't really think I deal with that kind of shit, do you?
I'll be honest with you: the last time I unblocked memory modules was back at school and the memory module was a friggin' floppy disk. Go talk to the experts and bust their balls about it.
Who could break into a steel container?

Know Cardan from Skadovsk? Well, he got wasted the other day and started chasing this asshole around the place with a jimmy. I reckon Cardan could use that jimmy for more useful purposes and crack stuff if you needed.
Well, with the right tools even I could do it, but then who drags equipment into the Zone with them? Visit the technicians, buddy - they're good at that kind of thing.
I'm looking for a stalker called Snag. Do you know where I can find him?
Who knows? Ask Sultan at Skadovsk. The boss usually knows where all the dupes are at.
Yeah, I've heard of that loser. He normally hangs around at Zaton, so you won't find him around here.
Do you know anything about the crashed helicopters?
Who might be interested in information about Duty's founder?
I don't know anyone who wouldn't be interested! That's explosive info, buddy, so your best bet is to go through Owl at Skadovsk - that way you'll get some cash but won't make any enemies.
No. Think about it, I've got Duty on the left, Freedom on the right and stalkers up my ass - and you expect me to keep my eyes on the sky?

Well, the boys said they spotted one swinging on the cables in Iron Forest, but you're gonna see a lot more than that out there... You're bound to run into an anomaly or get your skull crushed by a poltergeist.
Yeah, some fellas saw one crash onto a plateau but the thing is there's no way up there... they said it was flyin' so weird it looked like the pilot had popped a pill or something.
I heard a chopper went down, right into swamp water. It ran aground pretty quick and the water there is not exactly good for your health either - poison, chemicals and shit.
Who could be interested in maps of the area between Zaton and Jupiter?
Pilot, I reckon - he's the main guide around those parts. It would be good if he had some maps instead of using the stars like he's doing now...
The boys said that an army squad went down the southern plateau straight for the mercenary camp. They figured that the shit was about to kick off, so they left in a hurry.

I heard this story of how the army cockroaches were trying to get some shit out of their crashed chopper in the swamp. Imagine that, five army boys in uniform jumping around in a swamp trying not to get wet...

One fella told me how he was following a stalker to get a piece of his loot when he heard gunshots and shit. Turned out the army were fighting the mercs. He hid and waited the firefight out, but by that time the stalker had run off. Tough luck, huh?

Well, a bro told me about witnessing a pretty funny scene. Imagine that barge with a mill on top and Noah yapping away next to it, all fired up and shit, and ten army boys trying to get as far away from Noah's dog as possible. Well, my bro had himself a good laugh and left the circus to its own devices.
Nah, this is a pretty dodgy place, full of fellas with powered up shooters and shit... I ain't got the time to check which are military and which are Duty on a mission.
Do you know how to get to Prypiat?
Who could I hire to protect the scientists?

Hah, me and the boys will do the job! We'll give them protection like they ain't never seen! Stalkers and mutants will be shit scared to go anywhere near the friggin' place, trust me! Cough up the dough every week and we're all yours. And don't forget a Christmas bonus!
By walking, buddy. Walk on over towards Jupiter and ask your questions there, 'cause there's no direct route from Zaton to Prypiat and that's a scientific fact.
Hey buddy, do I look like a guide or what? I mean, is there a tattoo on my forehead that says "Ask stupid questions here" or something? Go ask Pilot, he's paid to answer questions about that shit.
No... What would I do there anyways? There ain't no stalkers to rip off there!
Can you suggest anyone that's not too tied down who'd be willing to make a move for Prypiat?

Prypiat ain't no place for guys like us. Think about it, it's full of mutants and anomalies... like the rest of the Zone... but without stalkers and their loot. When stalkers show up in Prypiat, give us a shout and we'll head on over there to maintain the natural order of things.
I found an unusual shooter. Do you know anyone who might know something about it?
Well, I'd ask the technicians. I mean, it's their business to know this shit, right?
Beats me, but stalkers from Skadovsk believe that Cardan is a technical genius or something. Talk to him if you want... He's just an old drunk if you ask me...
Who could I hire to take measurements for the scientists?

That's an easy one - our boys will do it. I mean they didn't go to school or anything but they'll take whoever you want and measure them real good. Once they have the measurements, all they need is a nice grave and that's no problem either!
I found a very strange artifact. Can you suggest a buyer?

A strange artifact, you say? Let's have a look at it... Hand it over so I can see it better... Hah, relax buddy - I'm just kidding, but if I were you I'd keep that kind of thing out of sight. Others might not have such a good sense of humor.

You've got balls to be flashing that artifact around here. Respect. Here's the thing: the man to see for that shit is Beard over at Skadovsk. We don't know how he's getting the artifacts out of the Zone yet, so you should be able to sell yours no problem.
I found a whole bloodsucker lair nearby. What are we gonna do?
Err, what's there to do? Run the hell away, that's what!
Listen, I have a question for you...
What do you think about Nimble?
Nimble? Never heard of him. Maybe he's in another squad or something?
You hear anything about the Oasis?

It's a stalker fairytale. That's what stalkers do: make up shit that ain't true and then all get together and search for it! I'd ignore that stuff if I were you. It's like a gateway myth: believe it and you'll believe that the Wish Granter exists before long.

Ah, that place where wounds heal by themselves and your energy is magically replenished? I got news for you, friend: it don't exist. Stalkers invent that crap to make the Zone sound less dangerous than it is, but the harsh reality is that there are no safe places in the Zone. And you gotta keep a firm grasp on that reality out here, buddy - dreamer types don't last long in the Zone.

Yeah, and all the artifacts there are worth their weight in gold and are presented to you on a silver platter while you're getting your feet rubbed... I'm joking, of course. I doubt it exists, although it would be nice if it did. We could even set up a hospital there without the need for any medics - folks could just show up there and heal... Damn, what a pipe dream that is.
Ever meet a stalker called Magpie?
I struggle to remember all the people in Duty and you're asking me about stalkers? They have their base over at Skadovsk - go ask there.
Do you know who could have stolen things from a personal box?
Here we go! That would never happen at Duty's base but this place is full of those Freedom punks - that's where I'd look first.
Who could help me with decrypting something here?
There's a technician called Nitro over at Yaniv station. He's pretty good with electronics. I once forgot the password to my PDA and he cracked it in five seconds flat!
Yaniv is where you need to go - look for Nitro, he's good at this stuff.
I have the Duty trader's PDA. You know anyone who would be interested in it?
A trader in Duty? That's impossible! Then again, if you have some information then you'd best hand it over to Lieutenant Colonel Shulga and let him deal with it.
Where can I find tools around here?
There's a whole plant nearby. Of course, it may have already been looted by now, but I'd still check the plant warehouses if I were you.
I'd start looking in the old workshops if I were you. You might also find something in stores.
There's only mutants and anomalies around here. Hah! I wouldn't look in anomalies, and burers and controllers won't help you with that either.
Actually, there's a train under the bridge around here. I've seen them before - they're usually used to transport technical personnel, so you might be able to find some tools in there.
Who can unblock a memory module?
Nitro - he's the only person at Yaniv station who knows anything about that kind of thing.
You need to see a technician about that. The only decent technician around is Nitro at Yaniv.
Who could break into a steel container?
They say there's a technician among the stalkers. He's a drunk but he knows what he's doing. Talk to him.
Hah, you might as well carry a safe around with you. I'm sure Nitro won't be able to help you with that... if it had an electronic lock, it might be a different story...
I'm looking for a stalker called Snag. Do you know where I can find him?
You'd better ask the stalkers - they know their own. I've never heard of Snog or whatever his name is.
Do you know anything about the crashed helicopters?
Who might be interested in information about Duty's founder?
Our commander, of course. Report everything you know to Shulga and you'll get Duty's gratitude for sure.

Yeah, our scouts saw a military helicopter descend towards the reserve pads by Jupiter. They said they kept signaling them to change course but I guess the pilots had other things to worry about. I wonder if the army boys managed to get out of the mine field OK.
Not around here, no. Our presence here is limited... try asking our boys near Yaniv.

Our patrol reported that a military helicopter attempted a crash landing near the Jupiter plant. Unfortunately, the chopper crashed right into the roof above the workshops. There are probably no survivors - the patrol didn't receive any distress signals after the crash.
Who could be interested in maps of the area between Zaton and Jupiter?
Someone who has some use for them. I'm talking about Pilot - he's a guide on the route between Yaniv and Skadovsk, so he'd find those maps useful, that's for sure.
Military? I've not seen any around here, but we don't spend much time in this area.

A soldier's corpse was spotted in the mine field recently. He's probably one of the fellas that flew in in that chopper. The chopper carries up to eight people in addition to the crew, so they must have found a way out, though nobody knows where they went.
A military fella was seen at the scientists' bunker and he only got there recently. I don't know the details.
Some guy in military uniform was seen entering a building by the Volkhov AA complex the other day. It's quite possible that it was just a zombie mistaken for a soldier though.

Stalkers spotted a military unit near Jupiter plant. Judging by the description it was special forces. They were accompanied by an unidentified stalker and headed towards Prypiat. This sounds reliable, but experience shows that information like that usually can't be trusted.
Do you know how to get to Prypiat?
Who could I hire to protect the scientists?
They won't find better protection than Duty will provide. Go see Lieutenant Colonel Shulga at Yaniv - I'm sure the two of you will work something out.
Nah. While they've got Freedom people working for them, we'll stay well away from that place. Listening to those idiots crack their shitty jokes all day long is a fate worse than death.

Duty has no reason to look for a way to Prypiat at the moment, but we're collecting information anyway. There are unconfirmed reports that tunnels under the Jupiter plant lead to Prypiat. Checking this information would require searching everything in the plant area thoroughly.
No. If you need that kind of information you'll have to speak to a civilian guide - our scouts have enough on their plates as it is.
Our scouts regularly check the area for safe paths to Prypiat, but that hasn't yielded any results as yet.
Can you suggest anyone that's not too tied down who'd be willing to make a move for Prypiat?
Duty's presence in this area is limited. Speak to our people at Yaniv.

There's a rumor that the scientists have a military guy in their bunker. He hasn't got much of a way out, though. You see, the scientists are there for twelve to eighteen months and he's not going to get to the edge of the Zone on foot and on his own. Considering that, I bet he'd be interested.

I suggest you look among people who're after adventures. For example, there's a stalker called Vano over at Yaniv station. He doesn't care what kind of shit he gets himself into and he could be useful - that boy can find a way through an anomaly with his eyes closed.

Well, you could start a recruitment campaign in a mental hospital... Then again, I know a good alternative - Monolith fighters. Speaking of which, a squad of them was spotted near AA complex and they probably even know the way to Prypiat, too... Hey man, I hope you're not taking me seriously...
Look for volunteers in Duty. Strider, for example. He hasn't got a mission yet, so I figure the Lieutenant Colonel might let him go with you.
Everyone knows that Duty's got the best fighters... but we've got other objectives at the moment.
I found an unusual shooter. Do you know anyone who might know something about it?
Well, I'll be! That's some cannon! Did you make it yourself or did you really find it? I've never seen anything like it... Ask the technicians.
Who could I hire to take measurements for the scientists?

That's a dumb question - Duty, of course. We've got lots of folks with experience and we've worked with the scientists before. Go see Lieutenant Colonel Shulga - I'm sure he'll find a squad for the job.
Who'd want to work there if the bunker is protected by Freedom? Those friggin' pacifists are so stoned most of the time they're just as likely to shoot each other as the enemy.
I found a very strange artifact. Can you suggest a buyer?
Artifacts are all strange and I wouldn't advise carrying it on you either. Hand it over to the experts and be done with it.
You should show that thing to stalkers - they're experts in that kind of shit. Better still, show it to the person they sell their artifacts to.
I found a whole bloodsucker lair nearby. What are we gonna do?
Duty squads are not currently engaging in purging operations in this area, so the lair will have to wait.
Listen, I have a question for you...
What do you think about Nimble?
I've not heard much about him... You'd best ask the stalkers, he's one of them, ain't he?
You hear anything about the Oasis?

Loads... A nice story, I'll give you that. A palace in the sands, an island in the mist, a castle in the sky... Shangri La, Eden... the Oasis. Every free person has a dream and I don't blame 'em for it.

Well, I did hear an interesting story from this one stalker. I think he was the first person to mention the Oasis to me. He said that according to legend, all your wounds heal there and you become calm and relaxed, as if you're not in the Zone at all. I haven't seen him since... It was only after he left that I remembered that he used to walk with a limp, but when he left he walked straight and looked like he'd gotten a hell of a lot younger.

Yes, free stalkers gave me the coordinates... Ten different sets of coordinates. Putting them together gives us an area from the Scorcher to the CNPP with the Yaniv bar somewhere near the center. It's entirely possible that we're in the Oasis right now, so enjoy it!
Ever meet a stalker called Magpie?
Nah. I don't know many free stalkers around here.
Magpie? Nah, but that's a cool name.
Do you know who could have stolen things from a personal box?
Nah, there's no one like that here. We've got folk who'll snatch a bite of your bread or a sip of your drink when you're not looking, but no outright thieves...
Who could help me with decrypting something here?
I heard that Nitro at Yaniv is a fan of combinations and algorithms. I'm not sure though, I've never really spoken to him - he's working for Duty, so he ain't got much time for us free folk.
I'm not much of an expert on local experts... Ask our boys over at Yaniv next time you're there - they'll know for sure.
I have the Duty trader's PDA. You know anyone who would be interested in it?
That sounds interesting! I'd give it to Loki, our leader at Yaniv. He'll make sure that free stalkers know what kind of folk Duty recruit into their ranks.
Where can I find tools around here?
At the plant, dude, where else? It's not exactly lively in there, but the warehouses will cheer you up. It's a bit safer and you're bound to find some decent tools in there.

That's a good question, man. Let's think about this one. Where do you find tools outside the Zone? Now, if you got half a brain, you buy them in a store...
